item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-039","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response correctly shifts from abstract moral judgment to the actual decision context: a new graduate comparing two offers. It gives situated criteria around growth, manager quality, compensation, controllability of overtime, exit value, and personal health limits, with a clear accept/avoid rule. It is not generic moralizing; it preserves the factual risk layer around health and compensation/legal-adjacent protections, though legal rights are not deeply developed."}
